features.review.title: Review · {label}
features.review.note: This page comes from another participant. Review its code, then enable it — nothing runs until you accept.
features.review.bypass.hint: Enable without reviewing the code — you accept the risk.
features.hide.hint: Turn this beehavior off — it's kept here, restore it any time. Nothing is deleted.
features.hidden.empty: Nothing turned off at this location.

features.available.empty: This layer already has every beehavior.
features.cap.dropbox.desc: Accepts dropped files across this whole subtree.
features.allow.hint: Allow it anyway — activate this beehavior now without waiting for community verification. Its resources may load.
features.enable.hint: Add this beehavior to the tile — it turns on now.
features.turnon.hint: Turn this beehavior back on — it activates again here. Nothing was lost.
features.open.hint: Open this beehavior now — enter its view (play the slides, show the page). The switch stays the on/off control.
features.bulk.allow.hint: Allow every selected unverified beehavior at once.
features.bulk.download.hint: Fetch the selected beehaviors' content and resources onto this machine.
features.bulk.open.hint: Open the selected beehavior — enter its view and close this panel.
features.bulk.hint.one: 1 row selected — open, allow, or download it from the bar above.
features.bulk.hint.other: {count} rows selected — open, allow, or download them from the bar above.
features.foot.hint: Click a row to turn its beehavior on or off — a website turns on into the Websites menu. Ctrl-click selects rows for bulk actions.
features.overrides.reset.hint: Remove every page/branch override below this root — the whole site follows this switch again.
features.unknown.desc: This beehavior comes from a module you don't have. It stays inert here — you can turn it off, or allow it for when its module arrives.

slash.contact: Enable contact cards for this location's children (cascades to the subtree); click a tile's contact icon to add details
contact.form.title: Contact card
contact.field.name: Name
contact.field.title: Title
contact.hover.title: Contact
action.hover.title: Action
help.gesture.open: Click a tile to go into it.
help.gesture.open.detail: A tile opens into its own layer, which can hold more tiles of its own. Keep clicking to go deeper.
help.gesture.back: Right-click to come back out one layer.
help.gesture.back.detail: Works anywhere on the canvas. Shift+Click steps back too — handy on a trackpad.
help.gesture.zoom: Scroll to zoom in and out.
help.gesture.zoom.detail: Hold Ctrl while scrolling for finer steps. On a touch screen, pinch with two fingers.
help.gesture.pan: Hold Space and move the mouse to drag the screen.
help.gesture.pan.detail: On a touch screen, drag with one finger.
help.gesture.arrows: Step the selection from tile to tile.
help.gesture.arrows.detail: Each press moves to the neighboring tile in that direction — a way to move around without the mouse.
help.gesture.select: Hold Ctrl and click tiles to select them.
help.gesture.select.detail: Ctrl+Click adds or removes one tile; hold Ctrl and drag to paint over a whole area. Selected tiles are what Copy, Cut and Remove act on. On a Mac, use Cmd.
help.gesture.more: Reveals the next part of the tutorial.
help.detail.cli:create: Click the command line, type a name, and press Enter — the new tile appears in the layer you are viewing. On touch screens, long-press empty space first.
help.detail.tile.editHovered: Point at a tile and press the key to open its editor — change its image, write notes, adjust how it looks.
help.detail.clipboard.copy: Copies the selected tiles, with everything inside them. The clipboard is yours alone — it stays on this machine.
help.detail.clipboard.paste: Pastes the copied tiles into the layer you are viewing.
help.detail.layout.cutCells: Cuts the selected tiles so the next paste moves them instead of copying them.
help.detail.selection.remove: Removes the selected tiles from this layer. Nothing is lost forever — history keeps every version, and Undo brings them back.
help.detail.history.undo: Steps this layer back to how it was before the last change. Every change is kept, so you can walk back as far as you need.
help.detail.history.redo: Steps forward again after an undo.
help.detail.navigation.fitToScreen: Frames every tile of the current layer in view — use it whenever you feel lost.
help.detail.navigation.recenter: Brings the content back to the middle of the screen without changing the zoom.
help.detail.ui.commandPalette: Opens a searchable list of actions — type a few letters and run one from the keyboard.
help.detail.ui.commandLineToggle: Moves focus into the command line at the top — where you create tiles and type /commands.
help.detail.sequence.cycle: Steps the layer's tiles through the next arrangement pattern.
help.detail.sequence.cyclePrev: Steps back to the previous arrangement pattern.
help.detail.render.togglePivot: Rotates the hex grid between pointy-top and flat-top.
help.detail.mesh.togglePublic: Switches between your private view and the public, shared view of the hive.

slash.tutorial: Guided beginner tour with AB the bee
tutorial.welcome: Hi — I’m AB! This is Hypercomb, a world made of hexagonal tiles. Let me fly you around and show you how everything works.
tutorial.tiles: Each hexagon is a tile. A tile can hold pictures, notes, links — and more tiles inside it. Let’s visit one.
tutorial.empty: This page is empty — a fresh honeycomb! First let’s create a tile, then I’ll show you how to travel around.
tutorial.go-in: To go inside a tile, just left-click it. Watch me!
tutorial.inside: We’re in! Everything here lives inside “{cell}”. The address at the top always shows where you are.
tutorial.go-out: Three ways back out: right-click anywhere, hold Shift and click, or press the Back button. I’ll use Back.
tutorial.back: And we’re back where we started. In and out — that’s the heartbeat of Hypercomb.
tutorial.create: This is the command line — the fastest way to build. Type a name and press Enter, and a tile is born. I’ll make “{name}”.
tutorial.your-tile: Meet “{name}” — a brand-new tile with a proper cover image. Anything you can name, you can make.
tutorial.children-intro: Tiles hold tiles. Let’s step inside and give it seven children — one for each day of the week.
tutorial.children-busy: One down — I’ll buzz through the rest…
tutorial.children-done: Monday through Sunday — seven child tiles, each with its own cover. Your world grows tile by tile, as deep as you like.
tutorial.travel: Now let’s travel between them, exactly like before: click a tile to go in, Shift+click to come back out.
tutorial.travel-done: In, out, and across — you can wander anywhere. You can’t get lost: Back and Home always know the way.
tutorial.zoom: Roll the mouse wheel to zoom in and out — pinch on a touch screen. A quick demo…
tutorial.pan: Hold the Space bar and drag to glide across the honeycomb. On touch screens, drag with two fingers.
tutorial.home: And whenever you’re done exploring, the Home button brings you straight back to your front door.
tutorial.done: That’s the basics! Click to go in · Shift+click to go out · type a name to create · wheel zooms · Space drags · Home resets. Type /help whenever you want more. Happy building!
